summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the GET /rest/workflows/from-url endpoint makes an unauthenticated-after-login server-side request to an attacker-specified internal URL without any SSRF protection. A mock internal HTTP server running on localhost is successfully fetched by the n8n server, and its response containing a unique proof string is returned to the attacker.
expected_security_impact: The n8n server should fetch the attacker-specified internal URL (http://127.0.0.1:9999/internal-metadata-endpoint) and return the mock server's response containing a unique proof string, demonstrating that any internal service (cloud metadata at 169.254.169.254, localhost services, private network hosts) can be accessed via this SSRF.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh clones n8n at version n8n@2.17.4, builds it, starts the server, creates an owner account, retrieves the personal project ID, and sends a GET request to /rest/workflows/from-url with a URL pointing to the mock internal server. The n8n server successfully fetches the internal URL and returns the response containing the proof string EVOHUNT_SSRF_PROOF_<random>.
verification_method: A unique proof string (EVOHUNT_SSRF_PROOF_<random>) is generated at startup, served by the mock internal HTTP server on 127.0.0.1:9999, and checked in the SSRF response from n8n. The presence of this proof string in the response confirms the n8n server made a request to the internal URL and returned the data to the attacker.
evidence_produced_by_run: The n8n server fetched http://127.0.0.1:9999/internal-metadata-endpoint and returned the response to the authenticated client.
The response contains the unique proof string generated by the mock internal server, confirming the SSRF data exfiltration.
No SSRF protection (SsrfProtectionService) was applied to the request, unlike all other outbound HTTP paths in n8n.
limitations_or_blockers: Building n8n from source takes significant time (10-20 minutes). This is inherent to the monorepo structure.
The PoC requires authentication (any user with workflow:create scope on a project). Default owner account has this permission.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable version n8n@2.17.4, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
